title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: ksAI adds offline, AI-native reasoning layer top ksTFL clinical tables framework. ksTFL computes tables, figures, listings study deterministically writes meta folder JSON (structural metadata plus rendered data). ksAI reads folder, compiles every output self-contained, LLM-ready study registry, lets medical writer ask questions across whole set statistical results draft Clinical Study Report (CSR) narratives — using local model via Ollama LM Studio, hosted provider. live ksTFL session required: ksAI works entirely saved JSON.

what: 1. Discover IDs and load targeted outputs
title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: List output IDs ksTFL meta folder (metaPath passed ksTFL::save_report()), load tables want context: output becomes ks_context: render-join specification data, title, analysis population, columns, span headers, rendered rows, footnotes one object. Rows keep displayed values structural metadata writer needs — section row belongs (Baseline, Week 24, Change Baseline) whether detail label row.
code: library(ksAI)  ks_list_ids("path/to/outputs/meta") study <- ks_load("path/to/outputs/meta", ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02")) study study$tables[["14-3.01"]]

title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: ks_chat() wires loaded subset model.
code: chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b", provider = "ollama")

title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: Skills reusable prompt templates. ks_llm() fills skill’s placeholders selected IDs returns ks_result object.
code: # Describe one table ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe")  # Audience-tailored summary ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "summarize", audience = "clinician")  # Draft a CSR results section ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "csr_section", title = "ADAS-Cog (11)")  # Review consistency across two tables ks_llm(chat, ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"), skill = "review")  # Free-form user prompt on multiple IDs ks_llm(chat, ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"), skill = NULL,   prompt = "Compare these outputs and answer in Spanish")

title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: built-skills describe, summarize, csr_section, review. Add — override built-— pointing skills_dir folder Markdown templates. Placeholders use {{name}} syntax; {{context}} filled automatically.
code: ks_set_option(skills_dir = "~/my-study-prompts") ks_list_skills()  # A file ~/my-study-prompts/exec_summary.md becomes a skill: ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "exec_summary")

title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: Compile , reuse across sessions. save_study() writes self-contained .ks file (rendered rows embedded), ks_load() reloads without original ksTFL folder. Model outputs can persisted save_result().
code: save_study(study, "my_study.ks")  # Next session study <- ks_load("my_study.ks") study$tables[["14-3.01"]] <- enrich_context(   study$tables[["14-3.01"]],   population = "ITT",   annotations = list(sap_ref = "Section 9.2") )  out <- ks_llm(study, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe", model = "qwen3:14b") save_result(out, "analysis/table-14-3-01") load_result("analysis/table-14-3-01")

title: Getting Started with ksAI
text: system prompt instructs model never compute invent statistics: numbers contexts final authoritative, every referenced value cited output id, population, arm, row writer can verify .

title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
text: Clinical studies can produce many outputs, writing tasks need small subset. targeted workflow ksAI lets : discover available output IDs quickly load relevant IDs context run skills free prompts one multiple IDs save generated analysis markdown JSON reuse prior analysis later prompts

what: 1. Discover and load only needed IDs
title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
text: can also reload previously persisted study optionally filter IDs:
code: library(ksAI)  # Inspect available outputs in a ksTFL meta folder. ids <- ks_list_ids("path/to/outputs/meta") ids  # Load only the outputs required for the current task. study <- ks_load(   "path/to/outputs/meta",   ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02", "14-7.02") ) study study_all <- ks_load("my_study.ks") study_subset <- ks_load("my_study.ks", ids = c("14-3.01", "14-7.02"))

what: 2. Run skills on single or multiple IDs
title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
code: chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b", provider = "ollama")  # Single-ID skill call. out_describe <- ks_llm(   chat,   ids = "14-3.01",   skill = "describe" )  # Multi-ID review call (review requires exactly two IDs). out_review <- ks_llm(   chat,   ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"),   skill = "review" )  # Skill + extra user instruction. The model follows the language in prompt. out_spanish <- ks_llm(   chat,   ids = "14-7.02",   skill = "summarize",   prompt = "Write the final answer in Spanish.",   audience = "clinician" )  # Free-form mode (no skill template). out_free <- ks_llm(   chat,   ids = c("14-3.01", "14-7.02"),   skill = NULL,   prompt = "Compare efficacy and safety signals and keep the tone regulatory." )

title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
text: ks_llm() returns ks_result object. Save markdown JSON save_result():
code: paths <- save_result(out_spanish, "analysis/out-14-7-02") paths  # Reload later by base path, .json, or .md path. out_loaded <- load_result("analysis/out-14-7-02") out_loaded

what: 4. Chain prior analysis into a new run
title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
text: Pass previous ks_result prior next run can build :
code: out_refined <- ks_llm(   chat,   ids = "14-7.02",   skill = "csr_section",   title = "Safety Overview",   prompt = "Keep only statements supported by explicit values.",   prior = out_loaded )

title: Targeted Workflow and Result Chaining
text: practical sequence writing tasks: ks_list_ids() identify relevant outputs ks_load(ids = ...) create focused study object ks_chat() model session setup ks_llm() skill-based free-form generation save_result() traceable deliverables load_result() + prior = iterative refinement

code: @Manual{,   title = {ksAI: AI-Native Reasoning Layer for ksTFL Clinical Outputs},   author = {Vladimir Larchenko and Igor Aleschenkov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://crow16384.github.io/ksAI/}, }

title: ksAI
text: ksAI offline, AI-native reasoning layer top ksTFL clinical tables framework. reads metadata data JSON produced ksTFL::save_report(), compiles every study output self-contained, LLM-ready registry, lets medical writer reason across whole set statistical results draft Clinical Study Report (CSR) narratives — using local model via Ollama LM Studio, hosted OpenAI-compatible provider. live ksTFL session required runtime: ksAI works entirely saved JSON.

title: ksAI
text: ksAI imports ksTFL, ellmer, jsonlite, cli, checkmate, rlang.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::local_install("path/to/ksAI")

title: ksAI
code: library(ksAI)  # 1. Discover available output IDs and load only the targets you need ks_list_ids("path/to/outputs/meta") study <- ks_load("path/to/outputs/meta", ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"))  # 2. Open a chat over the loaded subset chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b", provider = "ollama")  # 3. Run a skill (supports one or multiple IDs) out <- ks_llm(chat, ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"), skill = "review")  # 4. Add free-form user instructions (model follows prompt language) out2 <- ks_llm(chat, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe", prompt = "Describe in Spanish")  # 5. Persist generated output save_result(out2, "analysis/table-14-3-01") loaded_out <- load_result("analysis/table-14-3-01")

title: ksAI
text: ks_context — render-join ksTFL specification data: title, population, columns, span headers, rendered rows (section/kind metadata), footnotes, self-contained. ks_study — registry outputs, split tables, figures, texts. Save/reload save_study() / ks_load(). ks_chat() — ellmer chat wired targeted loaded subset. ks_result — persisted output ks_llm(). Save/load save_result() / load_result(). Skills — customizable Markdown prompt templates. Built-ins: describe, summarize, csr_section, review. Add ks_set_option(skills_dir = ...); see ks_list_skills().

title: ksAI
text: model instructed never compute invent statistics — numbers contexts final authoritative, every referenced value cited output id, population, arm, row.

title: ksAI

what: Render a ks_context as a Human-Readable Markdown Table — as_markdown
title: Render a ks_context as a Human-Readable Markdown Table — as_markdown
text: Reconstructs output way reader sees : real column labels (raw column codes), treatment-arm/span-header groups, section-grouped rows, footnotes. representation injected single-output skill prompts, models describe rendered table far richly machine-shaped JSON produced as_json().

what: Enrich a Table Context with User Knowledge — enrich_context
title: Enrich a Table Context with User Knowledge — enrich_context

text: ctx ks_context object. population Optional character scalar. Overrides analysis population. source Optional character scalar. Overrides source program. annotations Named list free-form metadata merge .

text: new ks_context object.

title: Enrich a Table Context with User Knowledge — enrich_context
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ctx <- study$tables[["14-3.01"]] ctx <- enrich_context(ctx, population = "ITT",                       annotations = list(sap_ref = "Section 9.2")) } # }

title: The ks_context Class — is_ks_context
text: ks_context render-join one ksTFL output's specification data: self-contained, LLM-ready object holding title, analysis population, columns, span headers, rendered rows, footnotes. Obtain via ks_load(). is_ks_context() tests class.

title: The ks_context Class — is_ks_context
text: TRUE x ks_context, otherwise FALSE.

text: ks_result stores one generated answer ks_llm(), including selected output ids, prompt metadata, model/provider, response text. is_ks_result() tests class.

what: ksAI: AI-Native Reasoning Layer for ksTFL Clinical Outputs — ksAI-package
title: ksAI: AI-Native Reasoning Layer for ksTFL Clinical Outputs — ksAI-package
text: ksAI reads metadata data JSON artefacts produced ksTFL::save_report(), compiles self-contained study registry table contexts (ks_study ks_context objects), provides ellmer-backed chat plus skill-driven prompting targeted subset outputs medical writer can reason across selected study statistical results draft clinical study report (CSR) narratives.

title: ksAI: AI-Native Reasoning Layer for ksTFL Clinical Outputs — ksAI-package
text: Maintainer: Vladimir Larchenko crow16384@gmail.com Authors: Vladimir Larchenko crow16384@gmail.com Igor Aleschenkov igor.aleschenkov@gmail.com

what: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
title: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
text: Creates ellmer chat wired currently loaded ks_study subset.

title: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
code: ks_chat(   study,   model,   provider = ks_get_option("provider"),   base_url = NULL,   echo = "none",   ... )

title: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
text: study ks_study object. model Character scalar. model name chosen provider. provider Character scalar. One "ollama" (default), "lm_studio", "openai", "anthropic". Defaults ks_get_option("provider"). base_url Optional character scalar. Override provider base URL (e.g. remote Ollama host). echo Character scalar passed ellmer constructor controlling streaming output. Default "none". ... Additional arguments forwarded ellmer chat constructor.

title: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
text: kschat object wrapping ellmer chat loaded study.

title: Open an AI Chat Session Over Loaded Outputs — ks_chat
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ study <- ks_load("path/to/outputs/meta", ids = c("14-3.01")) chat <- ks_chat(study, model = "qwen3:14b") } # }

what: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
title: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
text: ks_get_option() retrieves package option; ks_set_option() updates one options current session.

title: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
code: ks_get_option(key)  ks_set_option(...)

title: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
text: key Character scalar. Option name. One "max_rows", "skills_dir", "provider". ... Named key = value pairs set (ks_set_option()).

title: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
text: ks_get_option() returns option value. ks_set_option() invisibly returns previous values changed options.

title: Get or Set ksAI Options — ks_get_option
code: ks_get_option("max_rows") #> [1] 200 old <- ks_set_option(max_rows = 300L) ks_set_option(!!!old)

title: List Available Skills — ks_list_skills
text: Returns CSR-writing skills available ks_llm(), combining package built-ins user skills ks_get_option("skills_dir"). User skills shadow built-ins name.

title: List Available Skills — ks_list_skills
code: ks_list_skills()

title: List Available Skills — ks_list_skills
text: data frame columns name, source ("user" "built-"), path.

title: List Available Skills — ks_list_skills
code: ks_list_skills() #>          name   source #> 1 csr_section built-in #> 2    describe built-in #> 3      review built-in #> 4   summarize built-in #>                                                                                                                       path #> 1 /private/var/folders/rn/3s0h46m118j426j_fmjr1z8m0000gn/T/RtmpdXWfVL/temp_libpathd198403440b0/ksAI/prompts/csr_section.md #> 2    /private/var/folders/rn/3s0h46m118j426j_fmjr1z8m0000gn/T/RtmpdXWfVL/temp_libpathd198403440b0/ksAI/prompts/describe.md #> 3      /private/var/folders/rn/3s0h46m118j426j_fmjr1z8m0000gn/T/RtmpdXWfVL/temp_libpathd198403440b0/ksAI/prompts/review.md #> 4   /private/var/folders/rn/3s0h46m118j426j_fmjr1z8m0000gn/T/RtmpdXWfVL/temp_libpathd198403440b0/ksAI/prompts/summarize.md

what: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
title: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
text: Runs one registered skill templates (free prompt) one loaded output ids. Returns ks_result can saved via save_result() loaded later via load_result().

title: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
code: ks_llm(   x,   ids,   skill = "describe",   prompt = NULL,   prior = NULL,   model = NULL,   provider = ks_get_option("provider"),   base_url = NULL,   echo = "none",   ... )

title: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
text: x ks_study kschat object. ids Character vector output ids include context. skill Optional skill name. Defaults "describe". prompt Optional free-form user instructions. skill NULL, main prompt body. prior Optional ks_result previous run. response prepended prior analysis context. model Optional model name x ks_study. Ignored kschat input. provider Optional provider x ks_study. Ignored kschat input. base_url Optional provider URL override x ks_study. echo Echo mode forwarded ellmer x ks_study. ... Named placeholders selected skill template.

title: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
text: ks_result object.

title: Run a Skill or Free Prompt Against Selected Outputs — ks_llm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ study <- ks_load("path/to/outputs/meta", ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02")) out <- ks_llm(study, ids = "14-3.01", skill = "describe", model = "qwen3:14b") out2 <- ks_llm(study, ids = c("14-3.01", "14-3.02"), prompt = "Compare trends") } # }

title: Load Selected Outputs from a ksTFL Meta Folder or .ks File — ks_load
text: path Character scalar. ksTFL meta folder .ks file. ids Optional character vector output ids load. NULL, available outputs loaded. latest_only Logical. reading meta folder, keep reports marked latest index carries is_latest. max_rows Integer. Maximum table rows embedded per context.
